author: Da-Chuan Tian
description: Daily arXiv tracker that filters and summarizes papers via LLM into your vault. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Da-Chuan Tian
manifest.description: Daily arXiv tracker that filters and summarizes papers via LLM into your vault.
manifest.name: arXiv Daily

name: arXiv Daily

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Summary Language

- Added a Summary language setting with Chinese as the default and English as an optional output language.
- Applied the language setting to daily reports, detail summaries, and empty daily reports.
- Kept paper filtering unchanged so the language setting does not affect paper selection or topic classification.

### English Prompt Templates

- Added separate English Markdown prompt templates for daily summaries and detail summaries.
- Kept prompt bodies in prompt files instead of embedding them in TypeScript.
- Added partial-batch prompt templates for both Chinese and English daily summary runs.

### Indexing and CLI

- Updated daily summary parsing to recognize current Chinese labels and English labels.
- Added CLI/config support for `output.summaryLanguage` and `ARXIV_DAILY_SUMMARY_LANGUAGE`.
- Added validation for invalid summary language values.

### Release

- Published plugin assets: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tdccccc/arxiv-daily/compare/0.1.16...0.1.17

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Settings Simplification

- Removed advanced settings for temperature, request timeout, and lookback days.
- Removed skip/priority section settings and simplified section extraction behavior.
- Cleaned up dashboard actions by removing the paper mark shortcut from the More menu.
- Reordered dashboard actions so Summarize by ID and Run Pending are easier to reach.

### Prompt Improvements

- Unified prompt field names and improved prompt structure for daily and detail summaries.
- Removed redundant daily overview text from the daily summary prompt.
- Applied the same temperature cap to daily and detail summary generation.

### Reliability

- Fixed detail summary counting so existing detail notes are included in the nDetail count.
- Updated scheduler, extractor, summarizer, and CLI coverage for the simplified settings flow.

### Release

- Published plugin assets: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css`.
- Completed root and plugin release metadata for `0.1.16`.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/tdccccc/arxiv-daily/compare/0.1.15...0.1.16

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Overview

This patch release cleans up daily report generation, hardens Dashboard index sync, and fixes several reliability issues reported during Obsidian community review.

## Dashboard

- Reuse open Markdown leaves instead of opening duplicate tabs when navigating to the same paper or daily report.
- Display arXiv publication dates (published / updated) in the Dashboard paper index instead of local daily inclusion dates. Manual arXiv ID summaries now refresh existing index entries so previously wrong dates can be corrected.
- Refresh now reconciles `papers.json` against current daily and detail files; deleted or edited daily reports no longer keep phantom index entries alive.
- Prevent transient vault read failures from pruning existing paper index entries during history sync. Expired source cache directories are now cleaned up after file deletion.

## Daily Reports

- Remove legacy watch/highlight checkbox injection; newly generated reports no longer include Markdown status controls. The Dashboard and paper index remain the source of status changes.
- Keep daily report content scoped to selected papers — unselected arXiv candidates are no longer appended as a fallback list.
- Simplify paper detail frontmatter to reader-facing metadata only (title, authors, arxiv_id, primary_topic, daily_report, tags). Replace the old `date` property with a `daily_report` wikilink so Obsidian opens the correct report note.

## Plugin Reliability

- Remove runtime daily-selection sync that read legacy checkbox markers from daily reports. Dashboard and `papers.json` are now the single source of truth, preventing reload from clearing Dashboard stars.
- Fix run-state file saves to use temporary file + backup instead of renaming over an existing `run-state.json`, which caused "Destination file already exists!" errors in Obsidian.

## Validation

- All 267 tests pass (`npm test`).
- Build succeeds (`npm run build`).
- Version metadata aligned across root and plugin `manifest.json`, `package.json`, and `versions.json`.
- GitHub Actions release workflow passed for tag `0.1.14`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Overview

This patch release fixes the blocking Obsidian community review errors reported for `0.1.12` and republishes the plugin through the GitHub Actions release workflow so release assets include build provenance attestations.

## Obsidian Community Review Fixes

- Removed direct static style assignments from plugin UI code that were reported by `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment`.
- Moved the affected settings, modal, diagnostics, and ribbon styling into `styles.css` classes.
- Replaced settings-tab HTML section headings with Obsidian `Setting(...).setHeading()` entries, matching the community UI rule for settings headings.
- Kept the existing UI behavior for settings topic cards, diagnostics text, modal button rows, and the Dashboard ribbon placement.

## Release Provenance

- Updated the release workflow to generate GitHub artifact attestations for the Obsidian plugin release assets.
- The `0.1.13` release was built and published by GitHub Actions, including the attestation step before uploading assets.
- Release assets include `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css`.

## Validation

- GitHub Actions release workflow passed for tag `0.1.13`.
- `npm test` passed locally and in Actions.
- `npm run build` passed locally and in Actions.
- Verified version metadata is aligned across root `manifest.json`, plugin `manifest.json`, plugin `package.json`, and both `versions.json` files.
- Verified direct `.style` assignments and settings-page direct `h2` headings from the review error list were removed.

## Notes

The remaining Obsidian review items are warnings or recommendations, not blocking source-code errors. The direct filesystem, vault enumeration, and clipboard notices reflect intentional plugin capabilities used for cache/index management, vault-wide Dashboard indexing, and diagnostics copying.

releases.author: tdccccc
releases.description:
## Overview

This patch release focuses on Dashboard usability and performance for vaults with a growing arXiv history. The Dashboard now limits how many paper rows are rendered by default, while keeping search, filters, sorting, statistics, and paper metadata based on the full indexed result set.

## Dashboard Display Limit

- Added a new `Display` selector beside the `Sort` control.
- Available display sizes are `20`, `50`, `100`, and `All`.
- The default is now `20` rows, which avoids rendering every historical paper at once when the index becomes large.
- Added a result counter such as `Showing 20 of 238`, so it is clear how many rows are currently visible and how many matched the active filters.

## Behavior Details

- Search, topic filtering, date filtering, starred/detail-summary filters, and sort order still run against the complete Dashboard result set.
- The display limit only controls how many sorted rows are rendered in the table.
- `Select visible papers` now matches the visible table scope, so batch star/unstar/delete-summary actions are easier to reason about when only part of the result set is displayed.
- Choosing `All` restores the previous behavior and renders every matched row.

## Version Metadata

- Bumped the Obsidian plugin version to `0.1.12`.
- Updated root and plugin `versions.json` compatibility maps for Obsidian community plugin distribution.
- Release assets include `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` for manual installation or Obsidian community distribution scanning.

## Validation

- Ran the full plugin test suite: `npm test`
- Built the production plugin bundle: `npm run build`
- Verified version metadata is aligned across root `manifest.json`, plugin `manifest.json`, plugin `package.json`, and both `versions.json` files.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## arXiv Daily 0.1.11

This patch improves the first-run experience, Dashboard review, and scheduled run reliability.

### Added

- Settings now includes a Getting Started checklist for LLM, arXiv categories, research topics, and ready-to-run status.
- Dashboard empty states now give actionable next steps for setup, first run, pending runs, filters, and empty Starred views.
- Added bilingual Getting Started docs and linked them from the English and Chinese README files.
- Dashboard now has a Sort control for starred first, recently seen, published date, topic, and title.

### Fixed

- Dates newer than the newest arXiv /recent announce bucket now remain retryable instead of using submittedDate fallback. This avoids writing an incomplete approximate report before arXiv has posted the official announce-date page.
- Dates missing inside the current /recent window also remain transient; submittedDate fallback is reserved for explicit old backfills outside /recent.

### Validation

- npm test
- npm run build

Assets are attached for Obsidian installation: manifest.json, main.js, and styles.css.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- The Obsidian ribbon now opens the arXiv Daily Dashboard directly, making the Dashboard the primary entry point.
- The Dashboard is centered on a simpler Starred / All workflow with one-click star toggles for important papers.
- A compact daily-report calendar highlights today, marks dates with reports, and opens a report by clicking its date.
- Run controls are available from the Dashboard toolbar: Refresh, Run Today, Run Pending, and a More menu for lower-frequency commands.
- Paper titles, authors, and summaries in the Dashboard can now be selected and copied.

## Changed

- Removed the Dashboard Date field selector; From / To now filter by first-seen daily report dates.
- Removed citation / Zotero fields from the Dashboard path. Zotero remains the external source of truth for citation keys and BibTeX.
- Removed Add to project from Dashboard row actions.
- Row actions now focus on five paper operations: note, daily report, arXiv, open PDF, and download PDF.
- Root README, plugin README, and PLAN were updated to match the Dashboard-primary workflow and v0.1.10 direction.

## Community Directory Fix

- This release is tagged `0.1.10` without a `v` prefix so it exactly matches `manifest.json` version `0.1.10`.

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test

releases.name: v0.1.10

releases.author: tdccccc
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fix arXiv /recent date parsing for abbreviated English month names such as `Jun`, which caused June daily runs to fail with `date ... not in /recent (have: )`.
- Bump plugin metadata to `0.1.3`.

## Verification

- `npm test`
- `npm run build`
releases.name: v0.1.3

releases.author: tdccccc
releases.description:
## Headline

arXiv settings redesigned around a single **topics** list. Five legacy coupled fields collapsed into editable, collapsible topic cards with templates, validation, and a first-enable confirmation modal.

## Highlights

### Settings UX — topic-cards model
- `arxiv.topics: Topic[]` replaces `researchInterests` / `detailCriteria` / `detailCategories` / `categoryTagMap` / `categoryDisplayMap`.
- Each topic owns its name (heading text in the daily report), tag (auto-derived slug for Obsidian YAML `#tag`), description (LLM filter input), and per-topic detail toggle.
- Collapsible cards (one-line summary by default; click to expand).
- Five preset templates shipped in source: **Blank**, **Astrophysics + ML**, **NLP / LLMs**, **Computer Vision**, **Bioinformatics**.
- Empty-by-default: `DEFAULT_SETTINGS.arxiv.topics` is `[]` so a fresh install never silently filters for the author's own research areas.

### Onboarding & safety
- Config validation: API Key, Base URL, Model, and Topics all required.
- Red banner at the top of the settings panel lists what is still missing.
- `setScheduleEnabled` refuses to enable when the config is invalid.
- Manual commands gate on the same validation (filter config for Run today / Run all pending / Run for date; LLM-only for Summarize by ID).
- First-enable confirmation modal: **Run today / Skip today / Cancel**.
- New `skipped` `RunStatus` so the rest of today's interval ticks leave a user-skipped day alone.

### Help text
- Inline descriptions on first-time-setup essentials (API Key, Base URL, Model, Run time) and on each topic-card field.
- Circled `?` badge with Obsidian `setTooltip` on advanced fields (Temperature, Timeout, Tick interval, Request delay, Cache expiry, char limits, Skip / Priority sections, Log level).

### Migration
- One-shot, lossy migration from v0.1.x on first load: `detailCategories` ↦ `topics` (display names come from `categoryDisplayMap`, descriptions left blank). Free-form `researchInterests` / `detailCriteria` text is discarded — re-fill the description fields after upgrading.

## Tests
- 19 test files / 134 vitest tests passing
- `tsc -noEmit` clean
- Production bundle ~560 KB

## Install
- **BRAT:** Add Beta plugin `tdccccc/arxiv-daily` and update.
- **Manual:** download the three attached files into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/arxiv-daily/` and reload.
releases.name: v0.1.2

releases.author: tdccccc
releases.description:
## Changes since v0.1.0

### Features
- **Scheduler gating**: default disabled on fresh install; opt-in via Enable toggle
- **Skip existing files**: pipeline skips dates/papers already in vault (no LLM cost)
- **Status bar progress**: live pipeline stage + paper counter on Obsidian status bar
- **Provider presets**: LLM settings with DeepSeek/OpenAI/Anthropic/GLM dropdowns, auto-fill URL and models
- **Editable fields**: all LLM fields remain editable for any provider
- **Latest models**: OpenAI GPT-5.2–5.5, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7, GLM-5.1
- **arXiv category dropdown**: grouped by field (Physics/CS/Math/Stats)
- **Simplified arXiv config**: auto-generated tag/display maps, comma-separated categories
- **Timezone dropdown**: common presets + custom input
- **English UI**: all settings translated to English
- **Enable toggle at top**: shows Running/Paused status

### Fixes
- Writers throw on pre-existing files instead of silent overwrite (.bak removed)
- Pipeline pre-checks daily file existence before any network call
- Scheduler start() no longer fires immediate 5-day lookback
- Auto-generated category maps persist on settings render
releases.name: v0.1.1

releases.author: tdccccc
releases.description:
First public release of the arXiv Daily Obsidian plugin (v0.1.0).

## Features

- Daily arXiv fetch from `/list/<category>/recent?show=2000` with a 5-day rolling lookback
- Atom API abstract enrichment so the LLM filter sees full summaries
- OpenAI-compatible LLM (default: DeepSeek V4 Pro) for filtering, daily summaries, and per-paper detail reports
- Catch-up scheduler that runs while Obsidian is open
- Ribbon menu: Run for today / Run all pending in lookback / Run for specific date / Summarize by arXiv ID
- Per-machine run state (state machine: pending / running / completed / failed_transient / failed_permanent)
- Cross-platform (Windows / macOS / Linux)

## Installation

**Via BRAT (recommended):**

1. Install [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat) from Obsidian's Community Plugins
2. BRAT settings → Add Beta plugin → `tdccccc/arxiv-daily`
3. Enable **arXiv Daily** in Settings → Community plugins
4. Fill in your API key in Settings → arXiv Daily

**Manual:**

Drop `manifest.json`, `main.js`, and `styles.css` (attached below) into
`<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/arxiv-daily/`, then enable the plugin.

See [`plugin/README.md`](https://github.com/tdccccc/arxiv-daily/blob/main/plugin/README.md) for full usage and configuration details.

## Known limitations

- `failed_transient` retries are bounded by the tick interval; very busy days may need a manual "Run all pending" click
- arXiv occasionally rate-limits; the built-in 3-second throttle helps but is not a guarantee
- The plugin must be open in Obsidian for the scheduler to fire (catch-up handles the rest)
releases.name: v0.1.0 — first MVP release
